description: Shared content-slot core for the certified starter-template families. Every family's flat page frontmatter must stay a subset of this core plus its family overlay (CI: scripts/check-slot-manifest.mjs). Slots carry semantic descriptions, length bands, source policies, and proof/urgency flags so producers (copy-gen, image-gen) and the doctor share one contract.

provenance.note: Includes article_rating_text and urgency-conditional defaults from the proof-guards template PR (starter-templates #112).

non_family_dirs.description: src/ directories in the starter-templates checkout that are NOT template families (no manifest required). Anything else without a family manifest fails the CI subset guard.

source_policies.generated: Producer-filled (copy-gen/image-gen) from the brief payload and slot contract. Claims must trace to the claims whitelist or proof_assets.
source_policies.merchant_asset_required: Real merchant-supplied data or asset (review data, UGC, byline identity, product photography). Never generated; blank means the surface's guarded chrome stays absent (photo-less/section-absent variant).
source_policies.spec_truth: Copied verbatim from CampaignSpec/offer/store data (prices, guarantee, shipping, routes). Never composed by a producer (hard rule 5).
source_policies.brand: Merchant brand asset resolved via the template brand contract (logos, brand tokens).
source_policies.template_static: Template-owned chrome. Assembly never touches it.

flags.needs_merchant_input_able: When required input is missing, the producer routes this slot to needs_merchant_input instead of inventing a value; the builder UX (attestation/click-wrap lane) resolves it before publish.
flags.proof_surface: Slot renders proof (counts, ratings, reviews, UGC, identity). Content is governed by the negative-corpus anti-pattern classes; proof_modality mirrors the proof_assets modality vocabulary.
flags.enabled_by_verified_urgency: Urgency chrome (countdown/sell-out) renders only when the slot is non-empty AND the value comes from offer.urgency with verified:true. The doctor blocks rendered urgency without verified offer urgency.

Publication/outlet name in the byline row. Same identity rules as author_name: real and authorized or absent.

Estimated read-time chip, e.g. '6 min read'. Derive from actual article length.
Top promo-bar sale name, e.g. 'SPRING SALE'. Offer framing copied from spec/offer data, not invented.

slots.description: Badge pinned to the offer box border, e.g. the sale name. Spec-truth offer framing.
Small overline label inside the offer box between divider rules (all-caps).
Offer headline line 1 (uppercase), e.g. 'UP TO 60% OFF FOR'. Discount figure must be verbatim spec truth.
Offer headline line 2, rendered red (uppercase). Time-limited wording only from a verified promotion window.
Supporting sentence under the offer headline. No invented demand/stock claims.
slots.demo_value: This limited-time deal is in high demand and stock keeps selling out.

slots.description: CTA button label, e.g. 'GET 60% OFF'. Discount figures verbatim from offer data.
Funnel route the CTA advances to (page-kit route, usually landing or checkout). Owned by the Build Packet/CampaignSpec funnel topology.
Offer-box product image. Merchant-supplied product photography — product imagery is supplied, not generated (D20).

Label before the ticking countdown, e.g. 'OFFER ENDS IN:'. The countdown block renders ONLY when this is non-empty, and it may be filled ONLY from verified offer urgency (a real promotion window). Unverified → leave empty (hard rule 4).
LABEL half of the sell-out indicator, e.g. 'Sell-Out Risk:' — the field name shown to the shopper, not the value. Pairs with sell_out_risk.
VALUE half of the sell-out indicator, e.g. 'High'. The sell-out half of the bar renders ONLY when this is non-empty; fill only from a real inventory signal (anti-pattern 10: scarcity theater).

VALUE half of the shipping chip, e.g. 'FREE' — rendered bold before shipping_label. This is the value, not the label (canonical value-vs-fieldname confusion: do not fill with a shipping promise sentence).
LABEL half of the shipping chip, e.g. 'Shipping' — the field name rendered after shipping_text ('FREE Shipping').
Guarantee line by the CTA, e.g. '30 Day Money Back Guarantee'. Verbatim guarantee terms from spec truth — never a composed/rounded number.
Footer copyright line. Uses the merchant legal name ({{YEAR}} harness token supported).

Aggregate rating text beside the star glyphs, e.g. '4.8/5 (1,024 reviews)'. Real review-platform data only — never composed. Empty → the star-rating row does not render (the chrome is guarded). The adjacent star glyphs are fixed five-star chrome — only fill this slot when the real rating rounds to five stars; materially lower ratings need a star-count surface the template does not yet ship (roadmap).

checkout.note: Checkout keys are commerce wiring (bundles, shipping_methods, order bumps, MV variant slots) and diverge by design. The slot manifest references the commerce contracts instead of duplicating them; checkout content imagery is governed by the family brand contract.

roadmap.note: Corpus section-coverage audit (2026-07-22): sections winning campaigns use that the shared vocabulary cannot yet express, in descending revenue exposure. Proposed as shared-core additions — NOT forced into families whose templates lack the surface.

proposed_sections.notes: every logo requires a brief-supplied working URL (anti-pattern 8)
requires a brief-supplied comparison matrix and test record (anti-pattern 11)

proposed_sections.covers: CI-checked aliases for offer_*/cta_*/author_* mapper drift
